questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:[0, \infty) \rightarrow R$ be a continuous strictly increasing function such that $f^3(x)=\int_0^x t f^2(t) d t \forall x \geq 0$ then value of $f(6)$ is
questions.solution:
$f^3(x)=\int_0^x t \cdot f^2(t) d t$     ....(1)
Differentiate with respect to $x$
$3 f^2(x) \cdot f^{\prime}(x)=x \cdot f^2(x) \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{x}{3}$
$\Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{x^2}{6}+C$    ....(2)
But from $(1) f^3(0)=0 \Rightarrow f(0)=0$
$\therefore$ From (2) $C=0 \therefore f(x)=\frac{x^2}{6} \Rightarrow f(6)=\frac{6^2}{6}=6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)$ is a real valued function discontinuous only at $x=0$; having local extrema at $x=0$ and satisfying $(f(x))^2=4 \forall x$, then the number of function $f(x)$ is __________.
questions.solution: $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}2 ; & x=0 \\ -2 ; & x \in R-0\end{array} \quad f(x)= \begin{cases}-2 ; & x=0 \\ 2 ; & x \in R-0\end{cases}\right.$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\alpha$ is the absolute maximum value of the expression $\frac{3 x^2+2 x-1}{x^2+x+1} \forall x \in R$, then $[\alpha]$ is __________ (where [.] denotes the greatest integer function)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Let } y=\frac{3 x^2+2 x-1}{x^2+x+1} \\
& \Rightarrow(y-3) x^2+(y-2) x+y+1=0 \\
& \text { As } x \in R, D \geq 0 \Rightarrow(y-2)^2-4(y-3)(y+1) \geq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 3 y^2-4 y-16 \leq 0
\end{aligned}$
Clearly y lies between the roots.
Roots of the equation $3 y^2-4 y-16=0$ are $y=\frac{4 \pm \sqrt{208}}{6}=\frac{2 \pm \sqrt{52}}{3}$
$\therefore$ Absolute maximum of the expression $=\frac{2+\sqrt{52}}{3}=\alpha$ $\Rightarrow[\alpha]=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider a polynomial $P(x)$ of the least degree that has a maximum equal to 6 at $x=1$, and $a$ minimum equal to 2 at $x=3$. Let $P(2)+P^{\prime}(0)$ is equal to $n$ then $\left[\frac{n}{2}\right]$ is $\qquad$
questions.solution:
The polynomial is an everywhere differentiable function. Therefore, the points of extremum can only be roots of the derivative. Furthermore, the derivative of a polynomial is a polynomial. The polynomial of the least degree with roots $x_1=1$ and $x_2=3$ has the from
$a(x-1)(x-3)$
Hence $P^{\prime}(x)=a(x-1)(x-3)=a\left(x^2-4 x+3\right)$ since at the point $x=1$, there must be $P(1)=6$, we have
$P(x)=\int_1^x P^{\prime}(x) d x+6=a \int_1^x\left(x^2-4 x+3\right) d x+6=a\left(\frac{x^3}{3}-2 x^2+3 x-\frac{4}{3}\right)+6$
The coefficient ' $a$ ' is determined from the condition $P(3)=2$, whence $a=3$.
Hence $P(x)=x^3-6 x^2+9 x+2$
Now $P(2)=8-24+18+2=28-24=4$
Also $P^{\prime}(x)=3\left(x^2-4 x+3\right) \Rightarrow P^{\prime}(0)=9$
$\Rightarrow P(2)+P^{\prime}(0)=4+9=13$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)=3 \sin \sqrt{\frac{\pi^2}{9}-x^2}+|| x\left|-\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{2}\right|$, then the maximum value of $\frac{f(x)}{\sqrt{3}}$ is __________.
questions.solution:
Let $p(x)=3 \sin \sqrt{\frac{\pi^2}{9}-x^2}$ and $g(x)=|| x\left|-\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{2}\right|$. $\Rightarrow \mathrm{p}(\mathrm{x})$ and $\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})$ are even functions and $p(x)$ decreases in $x \in\left[0, \frac{\pi}{3}\right]$ and $g(x)$ decreases in $x \in\left[0, \frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{2}\right]$.
Hence $p(x)$ and $g(x)$ will be maximum at $x=0$ $\Rightarrow$ maximum value of $f(x)$ is $f(0)=3 \sqrt{3}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the function $f(x)=2 x^3-9 a x^2+12 a^2 x+1$ has a local maximum at $x=x_1$ and a local minimum at $x=x_2$ such that $x_2=x_1^2$, then $a$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$f^{\prime}(x)=6 x^2-18 a x+12 a^2=0 \Rightarrow x=a, 2 a$

Given $x_2=x_1^2$, so a cannot be negative. If $a\gt0$, then local minimum occurs at $x=2 a$ and local maximum at $x=a$. Thus $x_1=a$ and $x_2=2 a$ $\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{a}^2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=2$ (if $\mathrm{a}=0, \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})$ is always increasing)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The perpendicular from the origin to the tangent at any point on a curve is equal to the abscissa of the point of contact. Also curve passes through the point $(1,1)$. Then the length of intercept of the curve on the $x$-axis is $\qquad$
questions.solution:
Equation of tangent is $y-\beta=f^{\prime}(\alpha)\{x-\alpha\}$
Also, $\alpha=\frac{\beta-\alpha f^{\prime}(\alpha)}{\sqrt{f^{\prime}(\alpha)^2+1}}$
Thus, $\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{y^2-x^2}{x y}\right)$
Thus, equation of curve is $x^2+y^2-2 x=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find the smallest integral value of $P$ for which the function $f(x)=6 P x-P \sin 4 x-5 x-\sin 3 x$ is monotonic increasing and has no critical point on $R$ $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& f^{\prime}(x)=6 P-4 P \cos 4 x-5-3 \cos 3 x\gt0 \\
& 2 P\gt\frac{5+3 \cos 3 x}{3-2 \cos 4 x} \\
& 2 P\gt\max \text { value of } \frac{5+3 \cos 3 x}{3-2 \cos 4 x} \\
& 2 P\gt\frac{5+3}{3-2} \\
& P\gt4
\end{aligned}$
Smallest $P$ is 5
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Suppose a function $f:[0,10] \rightarrow R$ is continuous and differentiable everywhere in its domain. If $f(10)=19$ and $\left|f^{\prime}(x)-5\right| \leq 4 \forall x$ in domain. Find maximum value of $f(0)$.
questions.solution:
$$
y=f(x) \text { in }[0,10]
$$

Use L.M.V.T.
$$
\begin{aligned}
f^{\prime}(x) & =\frac{f(10)-f(0)}{10}=\frac{19-f(0)}{10} \\
-4 & \leq \frac{19-f(0)}{10}-5 \leq 4 \\
1 & \leq \frac{19-f(0)}{10} \leq 9
\end{aligned}
$$
$\begin{aligned} 10 & \leq 19-f(0) \leq 90 \\ -71 & \leq f(0) \leq 9 \\ \left.\therefore \quad f(0)\right|_{\max .} & =9\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Let $y=x^4+9 x^3+a x^2+9 x+4$ contain four collinear points then find the maximum value of $\left[\frac{a}{5}\right]$
$\qquad$ (where [.] denotes the greatest integer function)
questions.solution:
$\frac{d y}{d x}=4 x^3+27 x^2+2 x x+9$
$\Rightarrow y^{\prime \prime}=12 x^2+54 x+2 a$ should have real and distinct roots
$\Rightarrow D\gt0 \Rightarrow a \lt \frac{243}{8}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given two curves : $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$ passing through $(0,1)$ and $\mathrm{y}=\int_{-\infty}^{\mathrm{x}} f(\mathrm{t}) \mathrm{dt}$ passing through $\left(0, \frac{1}{3}\right)$. The tangents drawn to both the curves at the points with equal abscissas intersects on x -axis. Find the value of $\ell \mathrm{n} f(3)$.
questions.solution:
Equation the tangent to the curve $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$ is
$(\mathrm{Y}-\mathrm{y})=f^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})(\mathrm{X}-\mathrm{x})$
equation of the tangent to the curve
$\mathrm{y}_1=\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=\int^{\mathrm{x}} f(\mathrm{t}) \mathrm{dt}$ is
$\begin{aligned} & \left(\mathrm{Y}-\mathrm{y}_1\right)=\mathrm{g}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})(\mathrm{X}-\mathrm{x}) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(\mathrm{Y}-\mathrm{y}_1\right)=f(\mathrm{x})(\mathrm{X}-\mathrm{x})\end{aligned}$
Given that tangent with equation abscissa intersects on $x$-axis
$\therefore \quad x-\frac{y}{f^{\prime}(x)}=x-\frac{y_1}{f(x)}$
$\begin{aligned} \frac{f(x)}{f^{\prime}(x)} & =\frac{y_1}{f(x)} \\ \frac{f(x)}{y_1} & =\frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)} \\ \frac{g^{\prime}(x)}{g(x)} & =\frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)}\end{aligned}$
Integrating both sides we get, $\ell \mathrm{ng}(\mathrm{x})=\ln f(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{c}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \ell \mathrm{n}\left(\frac{\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})}{f(\mathrm{x})}\right)=\mathrm{c} \quad \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{k} f(\mathrm{x}) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{g}(0)=\mathrm{k} f(0) \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{k}=1 / 3\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=\int_{-\infty}^x f(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx} \\ & \mathrm{k} f(\mathrm{x})=\int_{-\infty}^x f(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx} \\ & \mathrm{k} f^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=f(\mathrm{x}) \\ & \frac{f^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})}{f(\mathrm{x})}=3 \\ & \ell \mathrm{n} f(\mathrm{x})=3 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{c} \\ & f(\mathrm{x})=\lambda \mathrm{e}^{(3 \mathrm{x})}\end{aligned}$
$1=\lambda\{\because$ curve passes through $(0,1)\}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=\mathrm{e}^{3 \mathrm{x}} \\ & \ln f(3)=9\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\forall h \in R-\{0\} 2$ distinct tangents can be drawn from the points $(2+h, 3 h-1)$ to the curve $y=x^3-6 x^2-a+b x$. Find the value of $\frac{a}{b}$ (where $a$ and $b$ are co-prime).
questions.solution:
$(2+h, 3 h-1)$ lies on the line $y=3 x-7$
This must be the line at point of inflection
$$
\begin{aligned}
y^{\prime} & =3 x^2-12 x+b \\
y^{\prime \prime} & =6 x-12=0 \\
x & =2
\end{aligned}
$$
$(2,-1)$ lies on the curve
$\Rightarrow \quad 2 b-a=15$ ...(1)
Also $\left.\quad \frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{x=2}=12-24+b=b-12=3$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad a=15 \text { and } b=15 \Rightarrow \frac{a}{b}=1
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:(0, \infty) \rightarrow R$ be a differentiable function satisfying $f(x)+e^{f(x)}=\frac{2}{x}-\ln x-1$. Find the number of integers in the range of $x$ satisfying the inequality $f\left(2 x^2+1\right)-f\left(x^2+5\right) \geq f(1), x>0$.
questions.solution:
$f(x)+e^{f(x)}=\frac{2}{x}-\ln x-1$ ...(1)
Differentiate both sides
$$
f^{\prime}(x)+e^{f(x)} f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{-2}{x^2}-\frac{1}{x} < 0 \forall x>0
$$

Hence, $\quad f^{\prime}(x) < 0 \forall x>0$
$\Rightarrow f$ is decreasing
Put $x=1$ in equation (1) $\Rightarrow f(1)+e^{f(1)}=1$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad f(1)=0 \\
& f\left(2 x^2+1\right)-f\left(x^2+5\right) \geq f(1), \quad f(1)=0
\end{aligned}
$$
$f\left(2 x^2+1\right) \geq f\left(x^2+5\right), f$ is decreasing
$$
\begin{aligned}
2 x^2+1 & \leq x^2+5 \\
x^2 & \leq 4 \\
-2 & \leq x \leq 2, \text { but } f:(0, \infty) \rightarrow R
\end{aligned}
$$
so the $x$ that satisfies the inequality belongs to $0 < x \leq 2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If the curve $f(x)=3 x^3+a x^2+b x$ where $a, b$ are non negative integers, cuts the $x$-axis at 3 distinct points. Find the minimum value of $(a+b)$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
f(x) & =x\left(2 x^2+a x+b\right) \\
D & >0 \\
a^2-8 b & >0 \\
a^2 & >8 b \\
\left.(a, b)\right|_{\text {min. }} & =(3,1)
\end{aligned}
$$

Note that $b$ can not be zero. (think!)
$$
\therefore \quad a+\left.b\right|_{\min .}=4
$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)$ is a differentiable function for all $x \in R$ such that $f(x)$ has fundamental period $2, f(x)=0$ has exactly two solution in $[0,2]$. also $f(0) \neq 0$. If minimum number of $\angle e r o s$ of $h(x)=f(x) \cos x-f(x) \sin x$ in $[0.99)$ is $120+\mathrm{k}$ then k is $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$h(x)=\frac{d}{d x}(f(x) \cdot \cos x)$
first find the minimum number of zeroes of $(f(x) \cdot \cos x)=0$
$f(x)=0$ has minimum 98 roots in $\{0,99)$
cosx $=0$ has 31 roots in [0,99)
maximum common possible root is only I
hence minimum number of roots of $f(x) \cos x=0$ is 128 .
Hence $\frac{d}{d x}(f(x) \cos x)=0$ has minimum 127 roots.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find the number of polynomials $P(x)$ with integer coefficients such that $P^{\prime}(x)>0$ and $(P(x))^2+4 \leq 4 P\left(x^2\right)$ for all $x$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& P^{\prime}(x)>0 \text { and }(P(x))^2+4 \leq 4 P\left(x^2\right) \\
& \text { Put } x=0 \Rightarrow(P(0))^2-4 P(0)+4 \leq 0 \\
& \therefore(P(0)-2)^2 \leq 0 \Rightarrow P(0)=2 \\
& \text { |||1y put } x=1 \Rightarrow(P(1)-2)^2 \leq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow P(1)=2
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Using Rolle's theorem in $[0,1] P^{\prime}(c)=0$ for some $c \in(0,1)$ but given $P^{\prime}(x)>0$. Hence no polynomials exists.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a thrice differentiable function $[a, b]$ and $\alpha < \alpha_1 < \alpha_2 < \alpha_3 < \alpha_4 < \alpha_5 < b$ and if $f(a)=f(b)=-2, f\left(\alpha_1\right)=f\left(\alpha_3\right)=3, f\left(\alpha_2\right)=f\left(\alpha_4\right)=-3, f\left(\alpha_5\right)=-1$, then what is the minimum number of roots of the equation $f(x) f^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)+f^{\prime}(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ for $x \in[a, b] ?$
questions.solution: $f(x)$ and $f^{\prime \prime}(x)$ may concide s. $f(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)$ has 5 distinct minimum roots $\therefore \frac{d}{d x}\left(f(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)\right)$ has minimum 4 distinct roots.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x^5-x^3+x=a$, when $x\gt0$, then the maximum value of $2 a-x^6$ is equal to _______
questions.solution:
We have $a=\frac{x\left(x^6+1\right)}{x^2+1}$
$\Rightarrow x^6+1=a\left(\frac{x^2+1}{x}\right) \geq 2 a$
$\Rightarrow 2 a-x^6 \leq 1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
There is a cubic polynomial $f(x)$ with values of $x$ lying in the interval $[-1,2]$. Given the condition.
(i) $f^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=24$
(ii) An extreme of $f^{\prime}(x)$ lies at $x=\frac{-1}{6}$
(iii) The coefficient of $x$ and $x^0$ in $f(x)$ are 0 and 6 respectively.

Find the greatest value of $f(x)$.
questions.solution:
Since the coefficient of $x$ and $x^0$ are 0 and 6 respectively, let $f(x)=a x^3+b x^2+6$. Then
$$
\begin{aligned}
f(x) & =a x^3+b x^2+6 \\
f^{\prime}(x) & =3 a x^2+2 b x \\
f^{\prime \prime}(x) & =6 a x+2 b \\
f^{\prime \prime \prime}(x) & =6 a=24
\end{aligned}
$$

Since $\quad f^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=6 a=24 \Rightarrow a=4$
Since an extreme of $f^{\prime}(x)$ occurs when $x=\frac{1}{6}$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad f^{\prime \prime}\left(\frac{1}{6}\right)=6 a\left(\frac{-1}{6}\right)+2 b=-4+2 b=0 \Rightarrow b=2
$$

Therefore $f(x)=4 x^3+2 x^2+6$ and $f^{\prime}(x)=12 x^2+4 x>0$ for $x>0$
Therefore $f(x)$ is an increasing function for $x>0$ and it is maximum when $x=2$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad \max (f(x))=f(2)=4\left(2^3\right)+2\left(2^2\right)+6=46
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the function $f(x)=2 x^3-(8-a) x^2+\left(a^2+\frac{16}{9}\right) x-12$ has a local minima at some $x \in R^{-}$, then find the number of integers in the range of $a$.
questions.solution:
Since point of minima is negative therefore point of maxima is also negative.
Hence, both roots of $f^{\prime}(x)$ must be negative and distinct.
Sum of the roots $ < 0$ and $D>0$
Their intersection is $\phi$, hence no values of $a$.

questions.q:
The equation $2 x^3-3 x^2+p=0$ has 3 distinct real roots then $[p]$ is $\qquad$
(Where [.] denotes the greatest integer function).
questions.solution:
Let $f(x)=2 x^3-3 x^2+p$
$\Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)=6 x^2-6 x=0$ at $x=0,1$
$\Rightarrow f(0)\gt0 \Rightarrow p\gt0$
$f(1) \lt 0 \Rightarrow 2-3+p \lt 0$
$\Rightarrow p \lt 1$
$\Rightarrow p \in(0,1)$
$\Rightarrow[p]=0$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A curve passes through $(2,0)$ and the slope of tangent at any point $(x, y)$ is $x^2-2 x \forall x \in R$. The point of minimum ordinate on the curve where $x>0$ is $(a, b)$, then find the value of $(a+6 b)$.
questions.solution: $$ \begin{aligned} f^{\prime}(x) & =x^2-2 x \\ f(x) & =\frac{x^3}{3}-x^2+c \\ f(2) & =0 \\ \therefore \quad \frac{8}{3}-4+c & =0 \\ c \quad & =\frac{4}{3} \\ f(x) & =\frac{x^3}{3}-x^2+\frac{4}{3} \end{aligned} $$ Minimum ordinate will be at $x=2$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad f(2)=\frac{8}{3}-4+\frac{4}{3}=0 \\ & \therefore \text { Point }(a, b)=(2,0) \\ & a+6 b=2 \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find the sum of all possible integral value(s) of 'a' for which $F(x)=\frac{x^3}{3}+(a-3) x^2+x-13$ has negative point of local minimum in the interval $[1,100]$.
questions.solution:
We have $F(x)=\frac{x^3}{3}+(a-3) x^2+x-13$
$\therefore$ For $F(x)$ to have negative point of local minimum, the equation $F^{\prime}(x)=0$ must have two distinct negative roots.
Now, $\mathrm{F}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{x}^2+2(a-3) \mathrm{x}+1$
$\therefore$ Following condition(s) must be satisfied simultaneously.
(i) Discriminant $\gt0$;
(ii) Sum of roots  < 0;
(iii) Product of roots $\gt0$
Now, D > 0
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow  4(a-3)^2\gt4 \\
& \Rightarrow (a-3)^2-1\gt0 \\
& \Rightarrow (\mathrm{a}-2)(\mathrm{a}-4)\gt0
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore a \in(-\infty, 2) \cup(4, \infty)...(i)$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Also } -2(a-3) \lt 0 \\ & \Rightarrow a-3\gt0 \\ & \Rightarrow  a\gt3...(ii)\end{aligned}$
And product of root(s) $=1\gt0 \forall a \in R$
$\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore & (\mathrm{i}) \cap(\mathrm{ii}) \cap(\mathrm{iii}) \\
\Rightarrow & \mathrm{a} \in(4, \infty)....(iii)
\end{array}$
Hence sum of value(s) of $a=5+6+7+\ldots \ldots . .+100=5040$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If tangent at a point $P_1$ (other than $(0,0)$ ) on the curve $y^2=a x^3$ meets the curve again at $P_2$. The tangent at $P_2$ meets the curve again at $P_3$ and so on, then find $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{i=1}^n x_i$, where $x_i^{\prime} \mathrm{s}$ are abscissae of $P_i$ with $x_1=3$.
questions.solution:
Let $P_1=\left(t_1^2, \sqrt{a} t_1^3\right)$ and $P_2=\left(t_2^2, \sqrt{a} t_2^3\right)$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \because 2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=3 a x^2 \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{3 a x^2}{2 y} \\
& \text { Slope of tangent at } P_1=\frac{3 \sqrt{a} t_1}{2}=\frac{\sqrt{a}\left(t_1^3-t_2^3\right)}{\left(t_1^2-t_2^2\right)} \\
& \Rightarrow 3\left(t_1\right)\left(t_1+t_2\right)=2\left(t_1^2+t_2^2+2 t_1 t_2\right) \\
& \Rightarrow t_1^2-t_2^2+t_1 t_2=0 \Rightarrow\left(t_1+2 t_2\right)\left(t_1-t_2\right)=0
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
t_1=-2 t_2
$$
$\therefore$ Abscissae, $t_1^2, t_2^2, t_3^2, \ldots . \Rightarrow t_1^2, \frac{t_1^2}{4}, \frac{t_1^2}{16}, \ldots$. will be G.P. with common ratio $\frac{1}{4}$ for $\forall a$.
$\therefore$ Sum of abscissae $=\frac{x_1}{1-\frac{1}{4}}=\frac{3}{3 / 4}=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=a_1 \sin x+a_2 \sin 2 x+\ldots+a_n \sin n x$, where $a_1 \in R$ and $n \in N$. If $|f(x)| \leq|\sin x| \forall x$, then the maximum value of $\left|a_1+2 a_2+\ldots+n a_n\right|$ is $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \left|a_1 \sin x+a_2 \sin 2 x+\ldots+a_n \sin n x\right| \leq|\sin x| \\ & \text { Let } f(x)=a_1 \sin x+a_2 \sin 2 x+\ldots+a_n \sin x \\ & \text { Given }|f(x)| \leq|\sin x|, f(0)=0\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x+h)-f(x)}{h} \Rightarrow f(0)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(h)}{h} \\ & \text { also }\left|\frac{f(h)}{h}\right| \leq\left|\frac{\sinh h}{h}\right| \Rightarrow|f(0)| \leq 1 \\ & \Rightarrow\left|a_1+2 a_2+\ldots+n a_0\right| \leq 1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider $f(x)=a x^3-b x^2+c x+d$,
where $\mathrm{a}=\frac{\cot ^{-1}(\cot 4)+\pi}{2}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{b}=\frac{3\left(\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} \operatorname{cosec} 6+2 \pi\right)}{2} \\ & \mathrm{c}=\left(\tan ^{-1} \tan 10\right)+3 \pi+2 \\ & \mathrm{~d}=5\left(2 \pi-\sec ^{-1} \sec 5\right)\end{aligned}$
If p represents the number of extremum of the function $f(\mathrm{x})$ and q represents the number of real roots of the equation $f(\mathrm{x})=0$, then find the value of $\mathrm{p}+\mathrm{q}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{a}=\frac{4-\pi+\pi}{2}=2 \\ & \mathrm{~b}=\frac{3(6-2 \pi+2 \pi)}{2}=9 \\ & \mathrm{c}=10-3 \pi+3 \pi+2=12 \\ & \mathrm{~d}=5\{2 \pi-(2 \pi-5)\}=25 \\ & f(\mathrm{x})=2 \mathrm{x}^3-9 \mathrm{x}^2+12 \mathrm{x}+25\end{aligned}$ $f^{\prime}(x)=6(x-2)(x-1)$ from graph $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{p}=2 \\ & \mathrm{q}=1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $A B C D$ and $P Q R S$ are two variable rectangles such that $P, Q, R$ and $S$ lie on $A B, B C, C D$ and $D A$ respectively and perimeter ' $\ell$ ' of PQRS is constant. If maximum area of ABCD is 32 , then $\sqrt{\ell}$ is equal to
questions.solution: Here $2 \mathrm{a}+2 \mathrm{~b}=\ell$ i.e. $\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}=\frac{\ell}{2}$ Area $(A)$ of rectangle $A B C D=A B \times B C$ $\begin{aligned} & =(A P+P B)(B Q+Q C)=(b \sin \theta+a \cos \theta)(a \\ & \sin \theta+b \cos \theta)\end{aligned}$ $=a b+\frac{\left(a^2+b^2\right)}{2} \sin 2 \theta$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \mathrm{A} \leq \mathrm{ab}+\frac{\mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2}{2} \text { or } \mathrm{A}_{\max }=\frac{(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})^2}{2}=32 \\ & \therefore \quad \frac{\ell^2}{8}=32 \text { or } \ell=16\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the functions $g(x)=x^2+a x+b$ and $h(x)=c x-x^2$ intersect and have the same tangent line at the point $(1,0)$, then find the value of $(b+c-a)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} g(x) & =x^2+a x+b \\ h(x) & =c x-x^2 \\ \Rightarrow \quad 1+a+b & =0 \quad \text { and } \quad 1-c=0 \\ c & =1 \\ g^{\prime}(1) & =m_1=2+a \\ h^{\prime}(1) & =m_2=c-2=-1 \\ g^{\prime}(1) & =h^{\prime}(1) \quad \Rightarrow \quad a=-3 \\ \Rightarrow \quad b & =2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
